answer.text: Orders from the same PAN on both buy and sell side are not matched against each other
The broker's proprietary orders don't interfere with client orders
Only one order per client is allowed at a time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Self-Trade Check prevents orders with the same PAN or CP code on both active and passive sides from creating a self-trade. If detected, the active or passive order is cancelled. **The Trap:** Self-trade prevention is at the PAN level, not just at the client code level. **Cross-Exam Context:** Pre-trade risk checks are tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Self-trades can be a sign of market manipulation (wash trades) and are actively monitored by exchanges.
